signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.49; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — 450bp carry differential supports USD/JPY. Watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7159; HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 7.3% — options pricing a 0.8 vol-pt premium over realized. CFTC LF net long +41,675 (week Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. Retail 88% short — contrarian signal. Watch for LF delta turning negative or IV skew to shift.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry premium; BoE expected to hold. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with carry. GBP/USD at 1.35 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV — options neutral.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Sentiment
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5909; retail 65% short — short bias but not extreme. NZD COT LF net short −17,090, long%=21.7% — not extreme positioning. Watch for NZD/USD break above 0.6000 to confirm bullish trend.
